behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file, the next() function, and describes the three behaviors tested: method forwarding, body forwarding, and streaming configuration (duplex).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the symptoms and what must be forwarded but doesn't give the exact code change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check fetch options (method, body, duplex) which are the standard Web Fetch API keys any correct fix would use.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests extract the template and execute the next() function via Node, capturing actual fetch arguments.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert method='POST', body='test body content', and duplex='half' in captured fetch calls; a stub would not pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three behaviors from instruction (method forwarding, body forwarding, streaming config) plus negative case (no body -> no duplex) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively (test_middleware_generates_method_forwarding etc.) with docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names appear consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points at the file and next() function which is natural for the task domain but doesn't leak exact lines.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not a config edit.
